channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — More than a week into what could be the costliest wildfires in U.S. history, weary and anxious Los Angeles-area residents were hoping Wednesday for a favorable shift in the winds and an end to the constant fear that their lives might be further upended. The blazes that began Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Montana House endorses transgender bathroom ban affecting fellow lawmakers
item.description: Montana’s Republican-controlled House on Wednesday endorsed a ban on transgender people using bathrooms in public buildings that do not align with their sex assigned at birth — a change that would complicate daily life for two fellow lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alabama inmate asks court to block nitrogen gas execution
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Attorneys for an Alabama inmate scheduled to be the fourth person put to death with nitrogen gas on Wednesday asked a federal judge to block the execution, arguing that the first three inmates showed signs of suffering from suffocation as the gas flowed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California Gov. Newsom slammed for 'sideshow' as he defends state's response
item.description: A California lawmaker is urging leaders to learn from their mistakes from previous California wildfires and unite to come up with a solution to wildfire prevention.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire tornadoes pose a threat in California. A pyrotechnics lab shows how they work
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter fire tornadoes — a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: For LA water issues, misinformation spreads nearly as fast as the wildfires
item.description: A billionaire couple was accused of withholding water that could help stop Los Angeles' massive wildfires. Democratic leadership was blamed for fire hydrants running dry and for an empty reservoir. Firefighters were criticized for allegedly using “women's handbags" to fight the fires.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South African police end mine rescue operation with at least 78 dead and 246 survivors
item.description: STILFONTEIN, South Africa (AP) — South African police ended a rescue operation at an abandoned gold mine on Wednesday and said they believe they have brought out all the survivors and retrieved all the bodies after hundreds of miners were trapped for months in one of the country's deepest mines whil
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bondi says she won't play politics as attorney general but doesn't rule out probes of Trump foes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi, Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, sought to reassure Democratic senators Wednesday that her Justice Department would not prosecute anyone for political purposes but also refused to rule out the potential for investigations into adversaries of the Republican presi
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles fire captain loses home to Eaton Fire
item.description: A man tasked with protecting the Los Angeles area from raging wildfires now ranks among those who have lost their homes and everything inside.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Actor Bill Hader 'in shock' returning to Palisades Fire neighborhood
item.description: "It's just gone. Everything," "Barry" actor Bill Hader said as he returned to the Pacific Palisades area.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Cases of HMPV are trending up in the US, CDC data shows
item.description: HMPV is considered relatively common, with most infected before age 5, according to Dr. Eileen Schneider, an epidemiologist with the CDC.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Craft retailer JOANN files for bankruptcy
item.description: Craft retailer JOANN filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy on Wednesday morning, marking the second time the company has done so in a year.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, WGN 9

item.title: Thompson, chair of Jan. 6 committee, has talked with White House about pardons
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.), who headed Congress’s special investigation into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, said Tuesday that he has been in talks with the White House about presidential pardons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: US recovers $31 million in Social Security payments to dead people
item.description: Ever since the U.S. Social Security Administration opened its books to the Department of the Treasury’s Bureau of Fiscal Service, it has been able to stop and recover more than $31 million in improper Social Security payments to dead people.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire deal, plan to free hostages as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to a ceasefire deal, mediators announced Wednesday, pausing a devastating 15-month war in the Gaza Strip and raising the possibility of winding down the the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Flags ordered to fly at full-staff on Inauguration Day in multiple states
item.description: GOP governors of at least eight states ordered flags to be flown at full-staff on Inauguration Day, bucking tradition by raising flags before the end of a customary 30-day mourning period following the death of former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: LA fires' heat, intensity driven by planet-warming pollution: Study
item.description: A major compounding factor was likely the downstream effects of two unusually wet winters the Southern California region saw in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KRON4

item.title: US in plea talks with Mexican cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. prosecutors said Wednesday that they're discussing a potential plea deal with Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, the long-elusive Mexican drug lord who was arrested last summer and whose son could testify against him if he goes to trial. Assistant U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfires latest: A final round of dangerous fire weather and dry conditions is in the forecast
item.description: Millions of Southern Californians were on edge as winds began picking up during a final round of dangerous fire weather forecast for the region Wednesday where two massive blazes have killed at least 25 and destroyed thousands of homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden to address the nation days before Trump's inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire tornadoes a risk under California's extreme wildfire conditions
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Michelle Obama signals she’s done with Trump
item.description: Michelle Obama is done with President-elect Donald Trump, if her decision to skip the inauguration means anything.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon man arrested, charged with murder in connection to missing person case from 2022
item.description: Police in Oregon say they have human remains believed to be of a man missing from 2022, and a suspect. Murphy Calvin Henry II, 54, is in custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live coverage: Bondi, Democrats clash during confirmation hearing
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump's pick to be the nation's next attorney general, on Wednesday clashed with Democrats during intense questioning at her confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Man in black Jeep wanted in California after stalking girls leaving school, police say
item.description: A man driving a black Jeep is being sought in Northern California after authorities say he stalked a group of girls leaving school on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione’s motive in alleged killing was ‘corporate greed’: TMZ
item.description: TMZ's Harvey Levin speculates that Mangione's alleged shooting stems from his frustration over "corporate greed.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Dire fire warning for LA area pushed back as winds ease
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Easing winds delivered a brief but much-needed reprieve to firefighters Tuesday as they battled two massive blazes burning in the Los Angeles area, and the National Weather Service pushed back its unusually dire warning of critical fire weather until early the following day.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5
